title: SDAP Handshake Init
description: The first message in the SDAP three-way handshake (Layer 2). Sent by the initiating agent to the target agent's SDAPHandshakeEndpoint to begin mutual authentication and session establishment. The initiating agent presents its DID, a nonce for freshness, an ephemeral X25519 public key for forward-secret key agreement, and a signature over the canonical message body using its Ed25519 authentication key.

type.description: Message type discriminator. MUST be exactly 'sdap/handshake/init'.

version.description: SDAP protocol version. MUST be '1.0' for this version of the specification.

initiatorDID.description: The DID of the agent initiating the handshake. The target MUST resolve this DID to verify the signature.

targetDID.description: The DID of the agent this handshake is addressed to. The receiving agent MUST verify this matches its own DID and reject the message if it does not.

nonce.description: A cryptographically random nonce, base64url-encoded (no padding), generated fresh for this handshake. MUST be exactly 32 bytes (43 base64url characters without padding). Used to prevent replay attacks; the responder MUST echo this nonce in the handshake-accept message.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which this message was generated. Receivers MUST reject messages with timestamps more than 300 seconds in the past or future (clock skew tolerance).

ephemeralKey.description: An ephemeral X25519 public key in JWK format, generated fresh for this handshake session. This key is used for ECDH key agreement (combined with the responder's ephemeral key) to derive the session encryption key via HKDF-SHA256. The private half MUST be discarded after session key derivation to ensure forward secrecy.

requestedScopes.description: Array of scope strings the initiating agent is requesting for this session. Scopes follow the pattern '<category>:<resource>:<constraint>' for namespaced scopes or use core SDAP scope names. The responder may grant a subset of these in the handshake-accept message.

requiredSecurityLevel.description: The minimum security level the initiating agent requires for this session. If the target agent cannot meet this level (e.g., it lacks Layer 4 delegation support required for 'high'), it MUST reject the handshake. Levels: 'basic' = Layers 1-2 only; 'standard' = Layers 1-3; 'high' = Layers 1-4; 'critical' = Layers 1-5 with HSM-backed keys.

signature.description: A compact JWS (detached payload) signature over the canonical form of this message. The canonical form is produced by serializing the message body (excluding the 'signature' field) using JCS (RFC 8785). The signature uses the initiating agent's Ed25519 authentication key, identified by the 'kid' header parameter referencing the key's DID URL.

x25519PublicKeyJWK.description: An X25519 public key in JSON Web Key format (RFC 7517 + RFC 8037).

kty.description: Key type. MUST be 'OKP' for Octet Key Pair (X25519/Ed25519).

crv.description: Curve name. MUST be 'X25519' for key agreement keys.

x.description: The base64url-encoded public key bytes (32 bytes = 43 base64url characters without padding).

kid.description: Optional key ID. For ephemeral keys, this may be omitted or set to a random identifier.

use.description: Intended key use. SHOULD be 'enc' for key agreement keys.
